text:
There once was a young, adventurous girl who loved chasing birds. Every day after school, she would rush through woods and meadows. One sunny afternoon, she spotted a beautiful teal bird dancing through the reeds.

She dashed over. The bird paused mid-flight. With a fluttery whistle, it started a graceful spiral arc toward her. She stopped just short, laughter bubbling up.

"Hello," she said. "Doesn't it feel fantastic?"

The bird did a little dance. "Probably."

She wrapped her arms around it. "Let's go somewhere quiet?"

The bird nodded eagerly. They flew down a gentle stream, then along a quiet country road. By a woods edge, the bird perched gracefully. She sat beside it.

"It makes me feel happy inside," she admitted.

The bird closed its eyes. "Do you think you could keep it? Talk to it?"

She nodded earnestly. "All the time."

Days turned into weeks. She learned about its song
